text: The surgeon isolates the renal loge and identifies the inferior vena cava, proceeding to dissect it. The main renal artery is then identified and clipped with Hemolock clips, effectively excluding the kidney from the blood flow.

The surgeon clips several lumbar veins to manage the inferior vena cava safely. Following the complete dissection of the inferior vena cava and the right renal vein, two vessel loops are placed around these structures.

The three-dimensional virtual model is overlapped onto the real anatomy using TilePro technology of the DaVinci Surgical Console. An assistant performs hand-assisted overlapping with a three-D mouse, allowing the surgeon to hide elements of the virtual model, such as the kidney parenchyma and caval wall, to better perceive the thrombus extension into the inferior vena cava.

The surgeon tightens vessel loops around the inferior vena cava, securing them with hemo-locks to interrupt blood flow. A cavotomy is performed on the anterior face of the ostium with the renal vein, revealing the tumor thrombus beneath the venous wall. The thrombus is gently extracted from the caval lumen.

The surgeon sutures the caval wall with a continuous 4-0 suture, securing it with a lapra tie after the thrombus removal. The vessel loops are removed, restoring venous return to the inferior vena cava. The procedure concludes with the completion of the radical nephrectomy.
